danger_signals.meaning: Pressure mechanic signaling danger of mission failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite damage state or health bar
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability and proximity to defeat

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear stage area with only background elements
Batman or Robin partner sprite on screen (co-op mode)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Movie-accurate costume design via motion capture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robin suit insignia (implied from film license)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Playable character with distinct digitized sprite
Multiple enemy types with distinct sprite designs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Digitized character sprites performing fighting animations in foreground
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Move sequences activate via button combinations; delayed punch/kick animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Background changes between levels; washed-out but distinct stage backdrops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player advances through level-to-level structure with gadget selection between stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gadget list UI selection screen (three standard + two custom per character)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must select gadgets before each level; impacts combat capabilities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two character sprites on screen simultaneously (Batman and Robin)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cooperative or competitive damage mechanics; shared or separate credit counts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fighting game arena environment (implied vs. level-based progression)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player fights computer or second player; no puzzle-solving or platforming

environmental_storytelling.example: Character animations reflect movie-accurate martial arts and fighting choreography; creates visual continuity with Batman Forever film
Progressive stages with distinct backgrounds communicate narrative progression through film adaptation content
Muted color palette in background graphics contrasts with vivid digitized foreground sprites, creating visual separation between player action and environment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs frame-delayed punch/kick sequences in response to button input; moves continue past enemy death state
Mirrors Batman combat mechanics with distinct digitized sprite animations
Respond to player attacks with animated sprite reactions; multiple distinct enemy types with variant behaviors
Cannot harm ally; share credit pool; coordinated against dual computer opponents
Can attack ally while managing enemy threats; separate credit tracking; independent survival

notes: Batman Forever SNES is a Mortal Kombat II engine-based beat 'em up with limited world-state communication compared to narrative-heavy titles. Visual communication relies heavily on digitized sprite animation fidelity and stage progression mechanics rather than dynamic environmental shifts. The game lacks sophisticated faction or political messaging—focus is on character-driven combat and film IP recognition. 'Washed-out' background graphics and sprite-foreground separation create visual hierarchy but limited narrative atmosphere. Co-op mode variants (damaging vs. non-damaging) represent the primary mechanical state differentiation affecting player perception.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Batman Forever SNES article)
